title: Punjabi Chole Masala

subs.name: 2 cans (400 g each) of chickpeas, drained; skip pressure cooking and add directly at step 5
1 tsp tamarind concentrate or juice of half a lemon for the sour note

ingredients.name: black tea bag
visual_checks: gives the chickpeas a darker colour
ingredients.notes: gives the chickpeas a darker colour

subs.name: not necessary — just cook the onion a little darker and use an extra pinch of coriander powder for colour

steps.text: Drain the soaked chickpeas. Pressure cook with 4 cups fresh water, 1 bay leaf, the black cardamom, cloves, cinnamon and the tea bag if using, for 5–6 whistles until the chickpeas are completely tender. Discard the whole spices and tea bag after cooking; reserve the cooking liquid.

Heat oil in a large kadai over medium-high flame. Add the cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add the sliced onion and the remaining bay leaf and fry, stirring often, until the onion turns deep golden brown — about 10 minutes. This dark colour is crucial for the final hue of the curry.

Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook until the raw smell lifts, about 2 minutes. Add the chopped tomatoes with a pinch of salt and cook on high flame, pressing them down with the spoon, until they completely break down and oil begins to pool at the edges, about 8 minutes.
Lower the flame. Add coriander powder, red chilli powder and turmeric; stir briskly for 30 seconds and splash in 2 tbsp chickpea cooking water if the masala sticks.
Add the cooked chickpeas along with about 250 ml of their cooking liquid. Stir well, crushing a small handful of chickpeas against the side of the kadai to naturally thicken the gravy.
Simmer on medium flame for 12–15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the gravy reduces and coats the chickpeas. Add the amchur and garam masala in the last 2 minutes.
Taste and adjust salt and sourness. Scatter fresh coriander over the top and serve with bhatura, puri, jeera rice or plain roti.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chickpea → 2 cans (400 g each) of chickpeas, drained; skip pressure cooking and add directly at step 5; amchur → 1 tsp tamarind concentrate or juice of half a lemon for the sour note; tea → not necessary — just cook the onion a little darker and use an extra pinch of coriander powder for colour
